description: Latency library for the opis-twin Monte Carlo simulator (GA_PLAN Phase 4). All distributions are lognormal, parameterized by p50/p95 in ms. confidence: 'sourced' entries cite a real published benchmark; 'llm-estimate' entries are model-proposed real-life expectations, explicitly unverified — replace with measured values when CA instruments real implementations. Norms are advisory end-to-end expectations keyed by base slot type, used by twin_check.py to flag requirement paths that are unusually slow — never a hard failure.

internet.source: Ookla H2 2025 mobile median RTT 44 ms (T-Mobile 5G NSA/SA mix); 4G LTE field measurements 30-50 ms median — https://enqt.de/en/blog/lte-vs-5g-part-2-latency-times/, https://spenza.com/telecom/5g-vs-4g-speed-comparison/
internet.note: one WAN traversal incl. mobile last mile; p95 covers congested/poor-signal tail

payment_processor.source: Adyen published commitment: 10k+ TPS with p95 authorization latency < 500 ms — https://www.index.dev/skill-vs-skill/payment-processing-mollie-vs-adyen-vs-stripe-europe; payment APIs generally expected sub-second p95 — https://nurbak.com/en/blog/api-response-time/
payment_processor.note: external card authorization round-trip, provider side

sentinel_auth.note: token validation / credential check, typically cached

producer_submitter.note: handoff to production system (kitchen display, fulfilment queue)

pickup_estimator.note: ETA computation with routing/traffic model call

driver_dispatcher.note: offer push + human driver acceptance; tail dominated by human reaction

payment_confirmed.note: payment confirmation: seconds, not minutes — user is watching a spinner

accepted_order.note: order acceptance feedback: a few seconds

command.note: dispatch command reaching a worker/driver: tens of seconds

estimate.note: ETA shown to user: a few seconds

event.note: bookkeeping facts: eventual, within a minute
